title: 1.1: Tests that WithCancel returns a context with a propagated logger that works after cancellation
1.2: Tests that WithTimeout returns a context with propagated logger and correct timeout behavior
1.3: Tests that WithLogger attaches a logger to a context and Logger() returns it
1.4: Tests that custom Context type satisfies context.Context interface and supports both logging and value retrieval
1.5: Tests that WithValue and WithValues propagate both context values and logger key-value pairs
2.1: Places the tests for context wrapper functions in pkg/context/
3.1: All tests use the Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test<FunctionName> or Test<Scenario>
3.3: All tests use testify assertions (assert package)
4.1: Uses infoCounterContext helper or equivalent logger callback mechanism for tests that verify logging behavior
4.2: Uses select with Done() channel and time.After for tests that verify context cancellation/timeout
